title: Missing files in new image after committing a container
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I have an ArchLinux image which I used docker to build due to #1867. I've then saved a tar for it, loaded it into rootless podman and squashed it with buildah. The new squashed image is working, all tools I've installed in it are there. Now, I've run this new image rootless and upgraded the system inside it with `pacman -Syu --noconfirm --removemake && pacman -Sc --noconfirm`. It upgraded an AUR package, starship-bin. All is fine while the image is still live, and `/usr/bin/starship` still exists. Now when I exit the container, and commit it, when I run the committed image, `/usr/bin/starship` is not there anymore, which means the image got corrupted, because pacman list starship-bin as an installed package, but now it's missing a file.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
❯ pacman -Qi buildah
Name            : buildah
Version         : 1.10.1-2
Description     : A tool which facilitates building OCI images
Architecture    : x86_64
URL             : https://github.com/containers/buildah
Licenses        : Apache

Depends On      : ostree  runc  skopeo  slirp4netns
Optional Deps   : None
Required By     : None
Optional For    : None
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 24.99 MiB
Packager        : Bartłomiej Piotrowski <bpiotrowski@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Sun 01 Sep 2019 04:45:27 PM -03
Install Date    : Sun 01 Sep 2019 07:02:25 PM -03
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : Signature
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
❯ buildah version
Version:         1.10.1

```

```

```

```
❯ uname -a
Linux leibniz 5.2.14-arch2-1-ARCH #1 SMP PREEMPT Thu Sep 12 10:42:38 UTC 2019 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
❯ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf

      autoextend_percent = ""
      autoextend_threshold = ""

      blocksize = ""

      directlvm_device_force = ""
```

comments.body:
This is the command output when I commit the just run archlinux container to a new archlinux image:

```
❯ podman commit archlinux archlinux
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob fb2e53f6804a skipped: already exists
Copying blob 4ec1da521752 done
Copying config d77e96cd6c done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
d77e96cd6c418e11fc8274cd583acb758a56769953adbd2c2142185ee42b9736
```

@oblitum Thanks for the problem report.  Just as a quick test if you've the time, can you try rerunning your test, but this time when you do the commit don't use the same name?  i.e. `podman commit archlinux myarchlinux`?

It's the first thing that comes to top of my mind, thanks.

I tried, problem isn't due to container/image name, same behavior. I just did the same steps with docker and found no issues. I just loaded the tar into docker, run, upgrade, exit, commit, run, all there, no missing files. I think I'll have to mix docker, buildah and podman in my workflow when I go about to do upgrades. It's quite convoluted. I have to load my image in docker, do an upgrade to it, then save it to a tar, then load it into rootless podman, and squash it with buildah.
Are you running root or rootless. Could you see if everything works when running podman/buildah as root.

I'm running podman rootless. Running podman as root on issue #1867 fixed that problem, but it may have been due because my root user isn't configured to use overlayfs. It also caused me to run out of disk space when I tried to run it as root for this current issue. I simply went to docker to fix my problem instead of setting overlayfs for root.

I'm sorry but I'm not interested in trying this as root again, as it's being quite burdensome, image is quite large, etc. I think I'll simply settle to operating upgrades and such operations in docker, and then simply saving to a tar and loading the image in rootless podman whenever I need to run it rootless.
